title: Poisoner silences the Empath
description: Poisoner must target Empath every night to suppress recurring info — tying up their ability permanently. If poisoned, you choose what number to show. Lean toward a number that contradicts the truth.

title: Poisoner corrupts Fortune Teller readings
description: A poisoned Fortune Teller gets a false yes/no. You can say 'Yes' to cast suspicion on a good player, or 'No' to hide the demon. Decide before waking them.

title: Night-1 poison neutralizes Washerwoman
description: If Poisoner targets Washerwoman on night 1, you fabricate the entire result. One-shot info wasted with no second chance.

title: Poisoner poisons Investigator for maximum misdirection
description: A poisoned Investigator gets false info pointing at evil. You can 'confirm' a good player as a Minion, devastating the trust network.

title: Poison breaks the Monk's protection
description: A poisoned Monk's protection fails silently — the protected player dies and Monk doesn't know their ability failed.

title: Poison makes the Soldier killable
description: A poisoned Soldier loses their protection and can die to the demon attack. If the Soldier dies at night, it proves a Poisoner was active.

title: Spy hides from the Empath
description: Spy registers as good to the Empath. If Spy neighbors the Empath, the Empath will always read 0 on that side even though an evil player is right there.

title: Spy deceives the Ravenkeeper
description: If the Ravenkeeper dies and chooses the Spy, you may show any character token. Use this to 'confirm' a player as good or cast suspicion elsewhere.

title: Spy's execution misleads the Undertaker
description: If the Spy is executed, you may show the Undertaker any character token — perfect for supporting evil bluffs after the fact.

title: Recluse becomes the Fortune Teller's red herring
description: Consider making the Recluse the Fortune Teller's red herring — they always get 'Yes' when checking Recluse, thinking they found the demon.

title: Recluse 'confirms' a good player as Minion
description: Recluse can register as the Minion shown to the Investigator, making the Investigator 'confirm' a good player as evil — devastating misdirection.

title: Recluse inflates the Empath's count
description: Recluse can register as evil to the Empath, giving a '1' even when both real neighbors are good. Creates doubt about Empath data.

title: Two demon survival paths
description: Imp can self-kill to pass demon to a Minion (starpass), OR Scarlet Woman activates if Imp is executed with 5+ alive. Two survival paths make this evil team very resilient — good must be precise about execution timing.

title: Baron+Imp: more Outsiders, fewer Townsfolk abilities
description: Baron adds +2 Outsiders (replacing 2 Townsfolk), so the Imp has fewer info roles to worry about and more disruptive Outsiders in the town.

title: Baron reveals its own existence to the Librarian
description: Baron adds Outsiders. Players can count Outsiders in play and compare to expectations — if they see extra Outsiders, Baron is likely. Librarian knowing about an Outsider confirms the count is off.

title: Assassin kills the unkillable Soldier
description: Assassin's one-shot kill bypasses ALL protection — including Soldier's permanent immunity. One of the most dramatic moments in BotC. Evil must decide if the Soldier is worth the one-shot.

title: Assassin bypasses Monk protection
description: Assassin kills through Monk protection. The protected player dies despite Monk's action — Monk doesn't know their ability was bypassed.

title: Assassin kills the unkillable Sailor
description: Sailor 'can't die' — but the Assassin's one-shot kill ignores this. The most reliable way to kill a Sailor. Evil must decide if burning the one-shot on the Sailor is worth it.

title: Assassin kills through Tea Lady protection
description: Tea Lady protects her alive good neighbors — but Assassin's kill ignores all protection. Can break the Tea Lady bubble with a single use.

title: Assassin wastes the Fool's one protection
description: Fool's first death is negated — Assassin kills force Fool to burn that protection. They are now fully vulnerable.

title: Double protection: Monk + Soldier
description: Monk actively protects one player per night, Soldier is passively immune. Evil has limited kills and now two sources of protection to overcome. Very strong for good without counter-roles.

title: Po triple-kill overwhelms single protection
description: Monk protects one player per night. Po's triple-kill targets three players — Monk saves one, but the other two still die. Protection can't keep up with the kill rate.

title: Po kills around Tea Lady's protection
description: Tea Lady protects her two neighbors. Po's triple-kill can target players beyond those neighbors, making the protection bubble less effective in high body-count nights.

title: Shabaloth can resurrect Professor's targets
description: Shabaloth may resurrect a previously killed player. If Professor also resurrects someone, the two resurrection mechanics can cause confusion about who is actually alive.

title: Exorcist stops Zombuul's conditional kill
description: Zombuul only kills when no one died that day. If Exorcist correctly picks Zombuul, no kill occurs that night — but this also means Zombuul doesn't get 'blocked' by a day death.

title: Pukka's delayed kill bypasses Sailor's protection
description: Pukka poisons tonight and kills the previous poison target tomorrow. Sailor poisoned by Pukka can die the NEXT night even if they chose a different player — because the kill resolves from the previous night's poison. Track the one-night delay carefully.

title: Pukka poisons Fortune Teller before killing
description: Fortune Teller poisoned by Pukka gets false readings the night they're poisoned, then dies the following night. One night of false info before elimination.

title: Vortox makes all Empath info wrong
description: Under Vortox, the Empath's recurring neighbor count is always false — you choose what false number to show. Sharp players will notice consistent false info and deduce Vortox.

title: Vortox makes all Fortune Teller info wrong
description: Under Vortox, all Fortune Teller readings are false. 'No' when one IS the demon, 'Yes' when neither is. The consistent wrongness is itself a clue — if every reading is wrong, Vortox is likely.

title: Vortox + Mathematician: the paradox
description: Vortox makes all Townsfolk info wrong, including Mathematician's count. If Vortox is the demon, the Mathematician's count is also false — which clever players can use as a Vortox confirmation.

title: No Dashii permanently poisons neighboring Empath
description: No Dashii poisons its two Townsfolk neighbors from the start. If the Empath neighbors No Dashii, they are permanently poisoned — giving false recurring info every night without any Poisoner involvement.

title: No Dashii poisons neighboring Fortune Teller
description: No Dashii's passive poison hits its Townsfolk neighbors. A Fortune Teller who neighbors No Dashii gives permanently false readings — with no obvious source of poison.

title: Vigormortis permanently poisons Tea Lady through dead Minions
description: When Vigormortis kills a Minion, one of their Townsfolk neighbors is permanently poisoned. If Tea Lady becomes that neighbor, her protection is gone forever. The Sailor can also be permanently poisoned this way.

title: Vigormortis permanently removes Sailor's protection
description: If Vigormortis kills a Minion who neighbors the Sailor, the Sailor becomes permanently poisoned — their 'can't die' protection is gone forever. The hardest counter to Sailor in the game.

title: Two character-swap sources: total chaos
description: Pit-Hag can change characters nightly; Barber swaps characters when it dies. Two independent character-swap mechanics in the same game creates extreme confusion about who is actually what character. Keep meticulous written notes.

title: Pit-Hag can change the demon mid-game
description: Pit-Hag can create a new demon from a non-demon player. If Snake Charmer is also present and swaps with a demon, the 'who is the demon' question becomes extremely complex.

title: Knowing the demon doesn't solve Evil Twin
description: If Demon kills Sage, Sage learns 2 players (one is the Demon). But the Evil Twin complicates the endgame — even knowing the demon player, the town might accidentally execute the Good Twin instead.

title: Double madness: overlapping mechanics
description: Both Cerenovus and Mutant involve madness. Ensure players fully understand what 'being mad' means before the game — having two madness mechanics active simultaneously can confuse new players.

title: Undertaker learns the Saint was executed — game over
description: If Saint is executed, good loses. The Undertaker would learn that night that the Saint was executed. But the game is already over. Consider how to communicate this outcome.

title: Butler triggering Virgin: an edge case
description: Butler can only vote if their master votes. But Butler CAN nominate independently. If Butler nominates the Virgin, the Virgin's ability triggers — Butler dies. Worth knowing if both are on the script.

title: Lunatic must simulate Po's triple-kill
description: The Lunatic thinks they're the demon and performs that demon's actions. With Po, this means simulating the charge-up mechanic and triple-kill. You must 'act out' a complex demon ability for a player who isn't actually the demon. Plan this before the game.

title: Lunatic must simulate Shabaloth's double-kill and resurrection
description: Shabaloth kills 2 and may resurrect 1. The Lunatic must simulate choosing 2 targets and the resurrection choice each night. Prepare fake results before running the Lunatic's turn.

title: Lunatic + Zombuul: simulate the false death mechanic
description: Zombuul only kills when no one died that day and can survive a 'first death'. The Lunatic must simulate this conditional kill mechanic — including potentially telling them they 'survived' a fake execution. Very complex.

title: Philosopher as Empath: double recurring info
description: If Philosopher takes the Empath ability, good has two Empaths running. However, the existing Empath (if in play) becomes drunk. And if Philosopher is poisoned, their Empath info is false.

title: Vortox makes all Philosopher-gained abilities wrong
description: If Vortox is the demon, any ability the Philosopher gains gives false info — same as any Townsfolk. This makes the Philosopher's one-shot investment potentially worthless.

title: Godfather kill after Saint execution ends both teams
description: If Saint is executed (good loses), Godfather can get an extra kill that night — but the game is already over. Interesting edge case if both are on script.

title: Tinker death can trigger Godfather's kill
description: Tinker might die at night (ST choice). If Tinker is the Outsider who dies, Godfather gets an extra kill — even though the death was ST-initiated rather than player-driven. Track day vs. night deaths carefully.

title: Mastermind + Zombuul: two layers of fake death
description: Zombuul survives their first death (registers as dead). Mastermind extends the game after the demon is executed. Combined: good might think they've won twice without actually winning.

title: Devil's Advocate and Exorcist both protect different roles
description: Exorcist protects from demon kills; Devil's Advocate protects from execution. Together they create two layers of protection for evil — demon survives night, demon survives day.

title: Witch curse + Virgin nomination: a trap
description: If a player is cursed by the Witch and then nominates the Virgin (who is a Townsfolk nomination trigger), the cursed player dies immediately from the Witch curse AND may trigger Virgin's ability — both happen.

title: True Gossip statement can kill the Soldier
description: A true Gossip statement kills a player — and the Soldier is not immune to Gossip deaths. The Soldier only survives the demon's attack. Gossip bypasses Soldier's protection.

title: Gossip kills can bypass Tea Lady's protection
description: Tea Lady protects neighbors from dying. But a true Gossip kill is ST-chosen — you can target a non-protected player, but if you choose a Tea Lady neighbor, their protection status matters.

title: Minstrel drunk triggers after Minion execution — during Godfather's kill
description: If a Minion is executed and Godfather gets an extra kill that night, ALL players may be drunk (Minstrel triggered). The Godfather's kill still happens, but it occurs in a drunk-everyone context.

title: Clockmaker's step count shifts with Evil Twin
description: Clockmaker measures steps from demon to nearest Minion. Evil Twin is a Minion — if the twin pair is near the demon in seating, the step count may be 1 regardless of other Minion placement.

title: Flowergirl tracks demon voting — Evil Twin complicates it
description: Flowergirl shows whether the Demon voted. Evil Twin complicates the deduction — even if Flowergirl confirms demon voted, the town still needs to separate the Good Twin from the demon.

title: Pukka's delayed kill can trigger Grandmother's death unexpectedly
description: Pukka poisons tonight and kills tomorrow. If Pukka's delayed kill lands on the Grandmother's grandchild, the Grandmother dies that same night — which may catch the town off guard since Pukka's kills come from the previous night.

title: Fang Gu + Barber: alignment and character chaos
description: Fang Gu can create a new evil Fang Gu from an Outsider. Barber can swap characters when it dies. Combined, tracking who is what character and alignment becomes very complex.

title: Courtier removes Sailor's protection for 3 nights
description: Courtier can target the Sailor CHARACTER — if in play, Sailor is drunk for 3 nights, making them fully vulnerable to the demon. Unlike Poisoner (must re-target nightly), Courtier is set-and-forget for 3 rounds.

title: Courtier drunks the Poisoner
description: Courtier can target the Poisoner CHARACTER directly — if Poisoner is in play, they are drunk for 3 nights and their poison has no effect. Removes evil's key misinformation tool for 3 full rounds.

title: Exorcist stops the Imp's kill
description: If Exorcist correctly picks the Imp, wake the Imp, show them the Exorcist token, point to the Exorcist, then put them back to sleep. No kill tonight. Very powerful when it hits — evil must avoid the Exorcist or kill them quickly.

title: Exorcist stops Pukka but previous poison still kills
description: Exorcist prevents Pukka from choosing a new poison target tonight. However, last night's poisoned player STILL dies — the kill resolves from the previous poison. Exorcist slows Pukka but doesn't stop an in-progress kill.

title: Spy can register as the Demon to Fortune Teller
description: At your discretion, the Spy may register as the Demon to the Fortune Teller. This can give a 'Yes' when neither player is the Demon, sending the Fortune Teller hunting the wrong person all game.

title: Spy distorts the Chef's neighbor count
description: Spy registers as good or evil at your discretion. If Spy sits next to the Chef, you can manipulate the evil-pair count. A Spy hidden as good can make a Chef '0' even when an evil pair exists.

title: Spy can register as any Minion to Investigator
description: Investigator learns 1 of 2 players is a specific Minion. Spy can register as that Minion, 'confirming' themselves as a particular type of evil — allowing them to control what role they appear as and misdirect the good team's suspicion.

title: Recluse inflates the Chef's evil-pair count
description: Recluse can register as evil to the Chef. If Recluse neighbors an evil player, the Chef may show '1' extra pair that doesn't really exist — distorting the seating deduction.

title: Recluse inflates the Oracle's dead-evil count
description: Oracle counts dead evil players each night. If Recluse is dead, they may register as evil — adding a false count. A '2' when only 1 evil is dead may suggest Vortox or be the Recluse.

title: Drunk Empath gives permanently false info
description: If the Drunk is placed on the Empath character, the 'Empath' gives false neighbor counts every single night with no way to detect it. One of the strongest drunk placements in TB.

title: Drunk Fortune Teller gives permanently false readings
description: A Drunk Fortune Teller receives false yes/no every night. Since they have a legitimate 'red herring' mechanic, false info is very hard to distinguish from intentional misdirection.

title: Drunk Virgin never triggers
description: If the Drunk is placed on the Virgin character, no Townsfolk nominator will ever die — the ability simply doesn't fire. This looks identical to an evil player nominating, which conceals the Drunk perfectly.

title: Vortox makes Oracle count wrong
description: Under Vortox, all Townsfolk info is false — including the Oracle's dead evil count. The Oracle gets a wrong number every night. A '0' when 2 evil players are dead is a strong Vortox tell.

title: Vortox inverts Town Crier's yes/no
description: Town Crier learns if a Minion nominated. Under Vortox, this answer is always wrong — 'no' when a Minion did nominate, 'yes' when no Minion did. Consistent wrong answers are Vortox evidence.

title: Vortox inverts Flowergirl's demon-vote info
description: Under Vortox, Flowergirl's 'did the Demon vote' answer is always false. If the demon never votes but Flowergirl always says yes, sharp players can deduce Vortox.

title: Vortox makes Juggler's count wrong
description: Juggler guesses character-player pairs and learns how many are correct. Under Vortox, the count is false — 5 correct guesses might show as 2. Juggler's one-shot is completely wasted if Vortox is in play.

title: Vortox inverts Seamstress's alignment answer
description: Seamstress asks if two players are the same alignment. Under Vortox, the answer is always wrong. If both players are clearly the same alignment but Seamstress got 'no', that's a Vortox tell.

title: Vortox gives Clockmaker a false step count
description: Clockmaker's step count from Demon to nearest Minion is false under Vortox. The seating deduction is completely wrong, potentially pointing good players entirely the wrong direction.

title: Vortox makes both Dreamer options wrong
description: Dreamer gets 1 good and 1 evil character — one is correct. Under Vortox, both shown characters are wrong. The player gets two incorrect options, making the Dreamer completely useless.

title: Vortox: Sage learns 2 players — neither is the Demon
description: If the Demon (Vortox) kills the Sage, Sage learns 2 players — one should be the Demon. Under Vortox, both players shown are wrong. Sage dies and their final info is useless.

title: Vortox flips Savant's true/false facts
description: Savant gets 1 true and 1 false fact each day. Under Vortox, the 'true' fact is now false and the 'false' fact may become true — the Savant's facts are both wrong, destroying the mechanic.

title: Vortox + Snake Charmer: the forced no-execution kills good
description: Vortox requires an execution every day or evil wins. Snake Charmer may hesitate to pick players because a Demon swap is risky. The pressure to execute every day constrains Snake Charmer's strategy.

title: Pit-Hag creates Vortox: instant all-info corruption
description: If Pit-Hag converts a player to Vortox, all Townsfolk info immediately becomes false from that night forward. The good team may not realize the shift happened, making all prior verified info suddenly wrong.

title: No Dashii poisons neighboring Oracle
description: No Dashii permanently poisons its two Townsfolk neighbors. If Oracle neighbors No Dashii, they give a false dead-evil count every night — permanently, with no Poisoner to blame.

title: No Dashii's poison shows in Mathematician's count
description: Mathematician counts ability malfunctions. No Dashii's passive poison means the Mathematician may consistently show '1' or '2' every night — confirming poisoning exists but not identifying the source.

title: No Dashii poisons neighboring Clockmaker
description: If Clockmaker neighbors No Dashii, they get a false step count on night 1. Since Clockmaker's info is one-shot, a poisoned reading is permanently wasted.

title: No Dashii poisons Sage — false demon pair
description: If Sage neighbors No Dashii and the demon kills them, Sage gets a false pair of players (neither is the demon). A Sage death under No Dashii gives actively wrong information.

title: Dead Witch (killed by Vigormortis) keeps Cursing
description: Vigormortis killed Minions retain their ability and poison a neighbor. A dead Witch still curses a player each night — even after execution or death — AND poisons an adjacent Townsfolk. Two disruptions from one dead Minion.

title: Dead Cerenovus keeps imposing Madness
description: A Cerenovus killed by Vigormortis retains their madness ability — they can still force a player to be 'mad' each night even while dead. The madness mechanic continues from the grave.

title: Dead Evil Twin (killed by Vigormortis) still wins
description: Evil Twin's win condition (Good Twin executed = evil wins, both alive = good can't win) remains in effect even if the Evil Twin is killed by Vigormortis. The dead twin still threatens the game state.

title: Dead Devil's Advocate still protects executions
description: A Devil's Advocate killed by Vigormortis retains their ability — they can still protect one player from execution each night, even while dead. Evil gets ongoing execution protection from a dead Minion.

title: Sweetheart's drunk target disrupts Empath permanently
description: When Sweetheart dies, you choose one player to be drunk forever. If you choose the Empath, their neighbor count is false for the rest of the game — no Poisoner needed, and it's irreversible.

title: Sweetheart's drunk target corrupts Fortune Teller readings
description: If the permanently drunk player is the Fortune Teller, all their future readings are false. The Fortune Teller can't tell if they're drunk or just got the red herring.

title: Ravenkeeper picks 'dead' Zombuul — learns their character
description: If the Ravenkeeper is killed and chooses the Zombuul who has already 'died' once, they learn the Zombuul — but the Zombuul appears dead. This can help good identify the still-active demon, or confuse them if they think the dead can't be the demon.

title: Fang Gu kills Goon — Goon becomes evil Fang Gu
description: Goon is an Outsider. If Fang Gu's first Outsider kill is the Goon, the Goon becomes an evil Fang Gu. The Goon (who changes alignment to match who targets them) now becomes an evil Demon — completely flipping their role.

title: Fang Gu kills Lunatic — the fake demon becomes real
description: Lunatic is an Outsider who thinks they're a Demon. If Fang Gu's first Outsider kill is the Lunatic, the Lunatic becomes an evil Fang Gu — the fake demon actually becomes the real demon. Extremely confusing mid-game shift.

title: Investigator may point at the Evil Twin pair
description: Investigator learns 1 of 2 players is a specific Minion. Evil Twin is a Minion — Investigator may 'confirm' one player as Evil Twin, revealing both twins' awareness of each other indirectly.

title: Undertaker sees the Evil Twin's character when executed
description: If Evil Twin is executed, Undertaker learns they were the Evil Twin. This confirms the good twin is still alive — which is valuable but also means good must now avoid executing the good twin.

title: Oracle counts Evil Twin as evil in the dead
description: Oracle counts dead evil players. If Evil Twin dies, they count as evil in the Oracle's total — useful confirmation that an evil player has died.

title: Klutz + Evil Twin: a lethal guessing trap
description: When Klutz dies, they must publicly choose a player — if evil, good loses. Evil Twin is evil. If Klutz points at the Evil Twin, good loses immediately. Evil should try to get Klutz to die when the Evil Twin is known to be in the circle.

title: Philosopher as Seamstress: a second alignment-check
description: If Philosopher takes the Seamstress ability, they get a bonus once-per-game alignment check. If Seamstress is already in play, they become drunk — but the Philosopher gets their one shot at the alignment question.

title: Philosopher as Courtier: on-demand 3-night drunk
description: If Philosopher takes the Courtier ability, they can drunk any character for 3 nights — on top of the real Courtier if present. This gives good two chances to drunk a critical evil character.

title: Philosopher as Snake Charmer: second demon-swap chance
description: Philosopher taking Snake Charmer's ability gets an additional nightly demon-swap attempt. If they pick the demon, the swap occurs — the Philosopher becomes the demon and is poisoned. High risk, high reward.

title: Pit-Hag moving the Demon invalidates Clockmaker's info
description: Clockmaker measures steps from the Demon to their nearest Minion. If Pit-Hag creates a new Demon in a different position, the Clockmaker's first-night info is now wrong — the demon has moved.

title: Pit-Hag creating new Minions triggers Town Crier
description: If Pit-Hag converts a player to a Minion who then nominates, Town Crier fires. But the town may not know a new Minion was created — the Town Crier signal looks like an existing Minion nominated.

title: Pit-Hag creates Fang Gu: a new Outsider-jumping demon
description: If Pit-Hag converts a player to Fang Gu, the new Fang Gu can jump to an Outsider on their first kill. Combined with Pit-Hag's existing chaos, now two characters can change who the demon is.

title: Barber swap under Vortox: swapped players get wrong info
description: If Barber triggers while Vortox is the demon, any character the swapped players now hold gives false info. A player becoming Empath under Vortox gets wrong neighbor counts — they don't even know they've been swapped into a broken role.

title: Spy tells the Imp exactly who to kill
description: Spy sees the entire Grimoire every night — including who Monk protected, who has what role, who's been poisoned. The Imp can coordinate with the Spy to pick kills that maximize impact and avoid protected players.

title: Exorcist identifying a Minion triggers Minstrel
description: If Exorcist picks the demon and good times an execution of a Minion the same day, Minstrel triggers — all players are drunk that night, including the newly exposed demon. No kills AND drunk night.

title: Jinx: Cerenovus + Goblin — madness creates real win condition
description: Djinn Rule: Cerenovus may make a player mad that they are the Goblin. If that player is nominated and executed while 'mad' (actively claiming to be the Goblin), the Goblin ability triggers and evil wins immediately.

title: Jinx: Poppy Grower + Lunatic — death reveals team info
description: Djinn Rule: When the Poppy Grower dies, Minions and the Demon learn each other. The Lunatic, who was shown fake Minions and a fake Demon setup on night 1, now also learns this real information — and may realize they are not actually the Demon.

title: Jinx: Pit-Hag + Vigormortis — instant neighbor poisoning
description: Djinn Rule: If Pit-Hag creates a Vigormortis, that Vigormortis immediately begins poisoning their two Townsfolk neighbors — even on the night of creation. The new demon's passive poison takes effect from the first night they exist.

title: Jinx: Lunatic + No Dashii — Lunatic poisoned by neighbor
description: Djinn Rule: The No Dashii poisons its two Townsfolk neighbors. If the Lunatic neighbors the No Dashii, the Lunatic is poisoned — their fake demon ability malfunctions. The Lunatic may pick different targets than their 'demon' actions suggest, confusing the real No Dashii.

title: Fortune Teller + Slayer: coordinated demon hunt
description: Fortune Teller narrows down who the demon is, and Slayer gets one kill on a suspected demon. If the Fortune Teller's read is right, Slayer can end the game instantly. Encourage these two to share info — the Slayer should know who the Fortune Teller suspects.

title: Empath narrows targets for the Slayer
description: An Empath with a 1 or 2 count knows their neighbors include evil players. The Slayer can aim their one-shot kill at the Empath's suspected evil neighbor — combining recurring info with a direct kill.

title: Investigator confirms Minion, freeing Slayer for the demon
description: Investigator reveals one Minion's location. If Slayer knows which player is definitely a Minion (not the demon), they can aim their kill more confidently at the demon rather than wasting it on a Minion.

title: Recluse wastes the Slayer's one-shot
description: Slayer shoots a player they think is the demon. Recluse can register as the Demon to the Slayer — you may allow the shot to 'hit' the Recluse and appear to fail. The Slayer loses their only opportunity.

title: Virgin + Slayer: two proactive good-side kills
description: Virgin can chain-execute a nominating Townsfolk, giving good an extra removal. Slayer provides a kill outside of execution. Two aggressive good-side tools together accelerate evil's exposure and give good more initiative.

title: Undertaker learns who Slayer killed
description: If the Slayer kills a player at night and that player is later executed, Undertaker doesn't see them. More directly: if good is tracking the Slayer's target, Undertaker can compare who died at night to clarify whether the Slayer hit or missed their demon guess.

title: Ravenkeeper + Fortune Teller: cross-reference demon suspicion
description: Fortune Teller builds ongoing suspicion about who might be the demon. If Ravenkeeper dies and picks a suspected player, confirming their character eliminates them from the demon pool or proves the Fortune Teller's read. Share info publicly.

title: Virgin chain-executions give Undertaker a burst of character info
description: Virgin's nomination-trigger can cause extra executions. Undertaker learns each executed player's character that night — when Virgin fires, Undertaker can gain multiple character confirmations in a single day.

title: Spy knows the Butler's master — controls the vote chain
description: Spy reads the Grimoire and knows who the Butler is and who their Master is. Evil can steer the Master's vote to manipulate the Butler into voting a specific way, effectively controlling two votes with one player.

title: Poisoner corrupts Chef's evil-pair cluster count
description: A poisoned Chef gets a false evil-neighbor count. You choose the number to show — either hiding evil clusters entirely or exaggerating them to send the town in the wrong direction.

title: Chef + Investigator: cross-reference evil-pair clusters
description: Chef reveals how many pairs of neighboring evil players exist. Investigator knows one Minion and a neighbor. Cross-referencing cluster info from Chef with the Investigator's specific seat narrows the evil seating pattern considerably.

title: Chef cluster map + Empath neighbor checks
description: Chef's count reveals evil clustering. Empath checks specific neighbors for evil. Together they triangulate: if Chef shows 2 pairs but Empath reads 0, evil is clustered far from the Empath's seat.

title: Poisoner can make the Saint a safe execution target
description: Saint loses the game for good if executed while good. If Poisoner has poisoned the Saint, their ability is broken — executing them won't trigger the Saint's loss condition. Track whether the Saint is poisoned before any execution is planned.

title: Drunk Chef gives a false evil-pair map from night 1
description: The Drunk thinks they are Chef but gets fabricated info. Their opening-night evil-pair count is wrong, sending the town's seating map astray from the very first piece of information shared.

title: Tea Lady + Sailor: overlapping protection bubbles
description: Sailor can't die from demon attacks. Tea Lady's alive good neighbors can't die at night either. A Tea Lady neighboring the Sailor gives the Sailor redundant protection AND makes Tea Lady's other neighbor invulnerable. Evil needs Assassin or poison to break through.

title: Monk shields Mayor for the 3-player win
description: Mayor wins if only 3 players remain and no execution happens. Monk can protect Mayor from the demon's kill on the final night, keeping Mayor alive for the bounce-win condition. The most reliable way to protect this win path.

title: Soldier survives for Mayor's final-three win
description: Soldier can't be killed by the demon. If Mayor, Soldier, and one other good player are the final three, the demon can't kill the Soldier to reduce the count — the Mayor bounce win becomes achievable.

title: Mayor needs no executions — Saint makes any execution catastrophic
description: Mayor's win requires skipping executions on the final day. Saint loses the game for good if executed. These overlap dangerously: a town that believes in the Mayor strategy will avoid executions, which also protects a hidden Saint. One wrong execution ends it.

title: Butler can't vote freely — silent support for Mayor's no-execution day
description: Butler can only vote if their Master votes first. With Mayor in play and the goal being no execution, a Butler whose Master doesn't vote can silently support the Mayor strategy — but evil will nominate aggressively to force a vote.

title: Chambermaid confirms Exorcist woke that night
description: Exorcist wakes each night. Chambermaid picking 2 players and including the Exorcist will always show '1' for them. This verifies the Exorcist is active, and if the count drops on a night they supposedly acted, something disrupted them.

title: Chambermaid confirms Innkeeper woke each night
description: Innkeeper wakes every night to choose 2 players. Chambermaid including Innkeeper always counts them as waking — verifying their claim and confirming nightly protection is happening.

title: Chambermaid tracks Gossip's nightly waking
description: Gossip wakes each night. Chambermaid can include Gossip in their 2-player check and always see them waking. Cross-referencing the count on nights when Gossip's statement may have triggered a kill adds useful context.

title: Chambermaid detects Po's triple-kill charging night
description: Po wakes each night, including the 'charging' night where they choose no one to prepare a triple-kill. Chambermaid seeing Po in their count on charging nights, combined with the following night's three deaths, can reveal the Po pattern.

title: Chambermaid can pinpoint the night Assassin used their ability
description: Assassin only wakes the one night they use their kill. Chambermaid seeing Assassin in their count on that specific night and not others reveals when the Assassin acted — useful for understanding night deaths.

title: No Dashii permanently poisons neighboring Chambermaid
description: No Dashii's passive poison hits its Townsfolk neighbors. A Chambermaid sitting next to No Dashii gets false waking counts every night — without any Poisoner involvement. The Chambermaid's info is permanently corrupted.

title: Vortox makes all Chambermaid counts wrong
description: Under Vortox, all Townsfolk ability info is false — including Chambermaid's nightly waking count. You choose what number to show. The Chambermaid's carefully tracked data becomes actively misleading.

title: Innkeeper + Tea Lady: layered protection bubbles
description: Innkeeper protects 2 chosen players from demon kills each night. Tea Lady's good alive neighbors can't die at night either. Overlap these two bubbles and evil must use Assassin or poison to get through — standard demon kills are blocked.

title: Innkeeper + Exorcist: two lines of demon defense
description: Exorcist stops the demon's kill entirely on a correct pick. Innkeeper protects 2 players on nights when Exorcist misses. Together they suppress most demon kills — evil needs both a missed Exorcist AND the demon to target outside Innkeeper's protection.

title: Innkeeper + Pacifist: night AND execution protection
description: Innkeeper protects from demon kills; Pacifist occasionally saves good players from dying when executed. Between the two, good players have multiple protection layers — day and night.

title: Assassin breaks Innkeeper's night protection
description: Innkeeper's protection blocks regular demon kills — but Assassin's one-shot kill ignores all protection. Innkeeper-protected players are still fully vulnerable to the Assassin. Save the Assassin for the most well-protected target.

title: Gambler dies wrong — Professor brings them back
description: Gambler dies if they guess a player-character pair incorrectly. Professor can resurrect one dead Townsfolk. If Gambler dies from a wrong guess, Professor can revive them — effectively letting the Gambler take two risky guesses across the game.

title: Gambler self-kill is a night death, not execution — Undertaker misses it
description: Undertaker learns the character of whoever was executed that day. A Gambler dying from a wrong guess at night is not an execution — Undertaker doesn't trigger. This asymmetry can create confusion about the source of the night death.

title: Gossip statement can coincidentally kill the Gambler
description: If Gossip makes a true public statement, a player dies at night. A Gambler who already died from a wrong guess that same night creates confusion about which kill source actually happened — or the Gossip kill may target the Gambler unexpectedly.

title: Exorcist stops demon, Gambler survives the attrition longer
description: When Exorcist successfully stops the demon's kill, no one dies that night. Gambler only dies from their own wrong guesses — fewer demon kills means the Gambler has more time to gather info and attempt guesses without external night-death risk.

title: Pacifist + Mayor: survive to the final three
description: Mayor wins if 3 players remain alive and no execution happens. Pacifist prevents some good-player executions from taking effect. Together they create a path to the Mayor win: Pacifist preserves good lives during the day, protecting the 3-player win condition.

title: Moonchild's execution kill bypasses Pacifist protection
description: Pacifist may save a good player from dying when executed. But Moonchild's ability triggers a SEPARATE kill on a chosen player when Moonchild is executed — this secondary death is not the execution itself, so Pacifist doesn't prevent it.

title: Mastermind protects Moonchild from execution death, Moonchild still kills
description: Mastermind keeps executed Minions alive until end of day. Moonchild is a Minion — if executed, Mastermind prevents their death, but Moonchild's kill ability still triggers. The town executes Moonchild, a player dies from Moonchild's ability, and Moonchild survives.

title: Moonchild + Godfather: double kills from one execution
description: When an Outsider dies, Godfather gets an extra kill that night. Moonchild is an Outsider — if Moonchild is executed, they trigger their chosen kill AND Godfather kills another player that night. One execution causes two deaths.

title: Moonchild kill vs Gossip kill — who caused the death?
description: If Moonchild is executed and Gossip's true statement triggers a kill the same day/night, the town sees a death but may not know which effect caused it. The ambiguity can obscure both the Moonchild and the Gossip mechanic.

title: Artist + Sage: two independent demon-hunting one-shots
description: Artist asks any yes/no question once per game. Sage, if killed by the demon, learns 2 players one of which is the demon. Two independent demon-identification tools — but Artist requires no death, while Sage requires being killed by the demon.

title: Clockmaker step count + Artist yes/no: narrow the demon's seat
description: Clockmaker learns the minimum seating steps between demon and nearest Minion. Artist can follow up with a targeted yes/no — e.g. 'Is the demon in the left half of the circle?' — to convert the step count into a specific seat range.

title: Artist + Mathematician: targeted false-info diagnosis
description: Mathematician counts players who received wrong ability info. Artist can ask 'Did player X get false info?' — combining the count with a pointed yes/no to identify who is poisoned and what the demon is doing to suppress info.

title: Vortox makes the Artist's one-shot question answer wrong
description: Under Vortox, all Townsfolk ability info is false — including the Artist's single yes/no question. The Artist will get the wrong answer to their one carefully chosen question, completely wasting their only use.

title: Flowergirl confirms if demon voted — Juggler narrows guesses
description: Flowergirl learns if the demon voted to execute today. Juggler makes character-player pair guesses and wants to hit the demon. If Flowergirl says 'yes, demon voted', Juggler can focus their guesses on players who voted — significantly narrowing the target pool.

title: Town Crier + Juggler: Minion-voter info for evil targeting
description: Town Crier learns if any Minion voted to execute today. Juggler wants to identify evil players. If Town Crier signals a Minion voted, Juggler can target those voters in their guesses — cross-referencing to identify both Minion and demon positions.

title: Oracle's dead-evil count tracks Juggler's hits
description: Oracle counts how many dead players are evil. If Juggler has correctly identified evil players who were then executed, Oracle's count rises — confirming the Juggler got hits and helping track how many evil players remain alive.

title: Dreamer + Seamstress: two alignment and character sources
description: Dreamer learns one player's character each night from two options. Seamstress learns if two players are the same alignment. Cross-referencing these: Seamstress can confirm whether the Dreamer's two character options are on opposite teams, resolving the uncertainty.

title: Mathematician calibrates whether Dreamer's info is trustworthy
description: Mathematician counts how many players received false ability info. If Mathematician's count is high, some Dreamer readings may be false — knowing the false-info rate helps players decide whether to trust the Dreamer's character options.

title: Philosopher as Sage: second demon-hunting death trigger
description: Philosopher copies the Sage. If the Philosopher-Sage is then killed by the demon, they learn 2 players one of which is the demon — a second Sage trigger. The demon must now choose: avoid killing both Philosopher and Sage, or give up powerful info.

title: Philosopher as Monk: second nightly demon protection
description: Philosopher copies the Monk, giving the team two independent demon-kill suppressors each night. Double Monk protection is very strong — evil needs an Assassin or poison to break through either protection layer.

title: Philosopher as Ravenkeeper: second death-info trigger
description: Philosopher copies the Ravenkeeper. If killed at night, the Philosopher-Ravenkeeper picks a player and learns their character — a second Ravenkeeper trigger. Especially powerful if the original Ravenkeeper is already dead.

title: Philosopher as Juggler: a second round of character guesses
description: Philosopher copies the Juggler. Day 1, the real Juggler makes guesses. Philosopher copying Juggler on night 1 can make their own guesses on day 2 — a second independent round of evil-player identification.

title: Witch curses Sage — punishes them for nominating after learning demon
description: Witch curses a player to die if they nominate. Sage who learns a demon candidate on death will want to nominate that player on their next day alive (if Prof-revived or context allows). Witch cursing Sage stops this: nominate and die, or stay silent.

title: Witch curses Juggler — stops the nomination after day-1 guessing
description: Juggler makes guesses on day 1 and gets results night 1. On day 2 they want to nominate their confirmed evil target. Witch cursing the Juggler stops this: nominating means death. The most info-rich player is silenced by the threat.

title: Cerenovus Madness disrupts Clockmaker's communication
description: Cerenovus forces a player to claim a specific character or risk dying. If Clockmaker is under Madness, they must act as a different character — making it very hard to credibly share their step-count information with the town.

title: No Dashii permanently poisons neighboring Juggler
description: No Dashii's passive poison hits its Townsfolk neighbors. A Juggler sitting next to No Dashii receives a false guess-count result on night 1 — their single most important piece of information is corrupted from the start.

title: Seamstress checks Evil Twin vs. their Good Twin — opposite alignment confirmed
description: Evil Twin and their nominated Good Twin are on opposite alignments. If Seamstress checks both twins, they learn they are 'different alignment' — which confirms one is evil. This doesn't reveal which is the Evil Twin, but narrows the evil pair significantly.

title: Snake Charmer may target the Evil Twin's seat near the demon
description: Snake Charmer can swap characters with the demon by choosing them. Evil Twin often sits near the actual demon to obscure seating patterns. If Snake Charmer is tracking demon-adjacent seats, they risk picking the Evil Twin instead — nothing happens, but the snake-swap fails.

title: Evil Twin voting triggers Town Crier detection
description: Town Crier learns if any Minion voted to execute today. Evil Twin is a Minion. If Evil Twin votes, Town Crier will see 'yes' — revealing a Minion voted. Combined with who actually voted that day, this can narrow the Evil Twin's identity.

title: Pit-Hag changes characters — Dreamer's accumulated info becomes stale
description: Pit-Hag can change any player to any character nightly, including creating a new demon. Dreamer has been building up character knowledge each night — but after a Pit-Hag swap, those identities shift. Previously reliable Dreamer info may now be wrong.

title: Poisoner + Vortox: zero reliable information in the game
description: Vortox corrupts all Townsfolk ability info globally. Poisoner adds targeted corruption independently. Combined, there is no reliable information anywhere — Vortox handles the global layer, Poisoner handles specific key roles. Good has no trustworthy data to work from.

title: Spy + No Dashii: passive neighbor poison + active role intel
description: No Dashii passively poisons its Townsfolk neighbors. Spy reads the Grimoire and knows exactly which players those neighbors are. Evil has both passive corruption of specific seats AND full knowledge of every good player's role — extremely powerful information control.

title: Exorcist + Sailor: two independent demon-kill suppressors
description: Exorcist stops the demon's kill entirely on a correct pick. Sailor can't be killed by the demon regardless. Two independent protections: if Exorcist misses, Sailor still survives. Evil must use Assassin, poison, or Vigormortis to reduce player count.

title: Shabaloth can kill the Grandmother's grandchild — triggering Grandmother's death
description: Grandmother learns their grandchild's identity. Shabaloth kills up to 2 players per night. If Shabaloth kills the grandchild, Grandmother also dies that same night — two deaths for the price of one kill, potentially eliminating key Townsfolk instantly.

title: Fool + Tea Lady: triple life protection overlap
description: Tea Lady's good alive neighbors can't die at night. Fool survives their first death attempt. A Fool neighboring Tea Lady has both protections — Tea Lady blocks the demon kill, and if bypassed (Assassin, poison), Fool has a backup life.

title: Professor resurrects + Sailor survives: attrition armor for good
description: Professor can resurrect a dead Townsfolk. Sailor can't die at night. Two complementary 'keep good players in the game' mechanics — Sailor prevents demon kills, Professor recovers from any kills that do happen. Evil needs Assassin to make progress.

title: Devil's Advocate protects a Minion from execution — Godfather follows up
description: Devil's Advocate lets one chosen player survive execution. If Devil's Advocate protects a Minion (or confusingly, a good player who the town is convinced is evil), Godfather can then kill that night from the outsider dying. Execution immunity and extra kill potential.

title: Washerwoman + Librarian: stacked opening-night info
description: Both roles give one-shot character confirmation on night 1 — Washerwoman names a Townsfolk, Librarian names an Outsider (or learns there are none). Cross-referencing the two pieces immediately builds a character map for the town. Very strong information start.

title: Washerwoman + Investigator: cross-reference opening info
description: Washerwoman names a Townsfolk and a player beside them. Investigator names a Minion and a player beside them. Cross-referencing both night-1 results triangulates the seating of TF, Minion, and Outsider from the very first night.

title: Washerwoman + Undertaker: opening identification then execution confirmation
description: Washerwoman identifies a character on night 1. If that player is later executed, Undertaker confirms their character — or reveals a discrepancy if Spy or Recluse was involved. Two-stage character verification chain.

title: Spy knows exactly who Washerwoman identified
description: Spy reads the Grimoire and sees what result the Washerwoman received — including the true character of the revealed player. Evil knows which good player was pinpointed and can prioritize targeting them or building a counter-narrative.

title: Librarian + Investigator: complete opening-night picture
description: Librarian names an Outsider, Investigator names a Minion (each with a neighbour). Together they cover Outsider and Minion identification on night 1, leaving only Townsfolk identification to the Washerwoman. Three-role opening is extremely powerful info.

title: Librarian can identify the Drunk — and the Drunk doesn't know
description: Librarian sees an Outsider on night 1. If the Drunk is in play, the Librarian might see them as the Outsider they are (not the Townsfolk they think they are). This identifies the Drunk for the town — a massive bonus since the Drunk's info is permanently false.

title: Recluse can fool the Librarian into a wrong Outsider reading
description: Librarian's result can include the Recluse as the 'Outsider'. The Recluse is actually a Townsfolk, so the Librarian's read sends the town looking for an Outsider that effectively doesn't exist — or confirms the wrong player as the Outsider.

title: Spy can register as any Outsider to the Librarian
description: Librarian sees an Outsider on night 1. Spy can register as any character — if Spy is shown as the 'Outsider', the Librarian has identified an evil player as an Outsider. This leads good to treat a Minion as a harmless Outsider.

title: Poisoner + Scarlet Woman: protect the demon, ensure succession
description: Poisoner protects the Imp by silencing key info-gatherers who might identify the demon. Scarlet Woman provides succession if the Imp is executed. Between poisoning critical roles and guaranteed demon survival via Scarlet Woman, the Imp is very hard to pin down.

title: Spy + Scarlet Woman: coordinated demon succession timing
description: Spy reads the Grimoire and knows when the Imp is in danger of execution. With this info, the Imp can decide when to self-kill (starpass) vs. rely on Scarlet Woman succession. Two coordination tools for demon survival in the same game.

title: Baron + Drunk: outsider count confusion deepens
description: Baron adds 2 real Outsiders to the game, replacing 2 Townsfolk. The Drunk is a Townsfolk pretending to be something else. With more real Outsiders in play from Baron, counting Outsiders becomes harder and the Drunk is even more concealed among the inflated pool.

title: Baron + Recluse: extreme outsider count misdirection
description: Baron adds 2 Outsiders. Recluse can register as an Outsider (or even Demon). If good tries to count Outsiders to detect Baron, Recluse can make the count look 'normal' by registering as an extra Outsider — hiding Baron's effect from the Empath, Chef, and visual counts.

title: Professor can resurrect the Tinker after a random death
description: Tinker might die at any time for no reason. Professor can resurrect one dead Townsfolk once per game. If Tinker dies unexpectedly, Professor is the natural resurrection target — recovering the lost player without spending an execution.

title: Tinker + Gossip: two unpredictable death sources
description: Tinker might die randomly at any time. Gossip's true statement also kills a player. With both in play, night deaths become hard to read — the town can't easily tell if a death was demon, Tinker randomness, or Gossip. Layers of ambiguity around who killed whom.

title: Tea Lady protects neighbors — but not from Tinker's random death
description: Tea Lady's alive good neighbors can't die at night from demon kills. Tinker's 'might die at any time' is a separate mechanic that bypasses this protection. If Tinker neighbors Tea Lady, the Tea Lady cannot stop Tinker's possible random death.

title: Exorcist stops the demon from targeting Goon — preventing alignment flip
description: Goon flips to evil alignment when chosen by the demon. Exorcist who picks the demon on the same night the demon targets Goon prevents the kill/choice entirely — Goon stays good. Strong combo when you suspect the demon will try to flip the Goon.

title: Goon + Lunatic: both mirror and distort demon targeting
description: Lunatic thinks they are the demon and mimics demon choices. Goon flips alignment when chosen by the demon. If the Lunatic 'chooses' the Goon (thinking they're the demon), the Goon is NOT flipped — only the real demon triggers Goon. A subtle signal for deduction.

title: Goon + Sailor: two roles evil struggles to kill normally
description: Sailor can't die from demon attacks. Goon flips to evil if the demon targets them (rather than dying). Both are difficult for evil to neutralize without special tools — Assassin, Vigormortis poison, or accepting a flipped Goon. Strong defensive pair.

title: Spy warns the demon who the Goon is — demon avoids flipping them
description: Spy reads the Grimoire and knows who the Goon is. Evil can warn the demon to never choose the Goon (since a demon-chosen Goon flips evil, giving good a free agent). Without Spy, the demon risks accidentally flipping the Goon against their will.

title: Spy executed triggers Minstrel's global drunk
description: Minstrel drunkens all Townsfolk for a day+night when a Minion is executed. Spy is a Minion. If Spy is executed, Minstrel fires — giving evil a bonus round of false Townsfolk ability info even after losing the Spy. A parting gift from the Spy.

title: Minstrel fires when Minion executed — Scarlet Woman ready if Imp falls
description: If a Minion is executed and Minstrel fires, all Townsfolk are drunk. If the Imp dies during that drunk window (from nomination or other cause), Scarlet Woman becomes the new demon while everyone's information is corrupted. A catastrophic combination for good.

title: Fool + Monk: two layers of protection against the demon
description: Monk actively protects one player per night. Fool's first death is negated. If the demon targets the Fool on a Monk-protected night, the protection is redundant but covers both failure cases. If Monk is wrong, Fool still survives their first death.

title: Pukka's two-stage kill wastes then kills the Fool
description: Pukka poisons tonight and kills the poisoned player the next night. If Fool is poisoned by Pukka, they die the next night — their first death. Fool's protection triggers, they survive. But next night Pukka poisons them again, and the following night kills them for real. Two-night setup to bypass the Fool.

title: Mastermind keeps the Imp alive after execution
description: If the Imp is executed, Mastermind keeps them alive until end of day. The Imp doesn't die, so they don't starpass and Scarlet Woman doesn't activate. Evil retains their demon — but Mastermind is then spent. High-value save for the most important moment.

title: Mastermind saves Imp, so Scarlet Woman never activates
description: Mastermind keeps executed Minions (and the Imp) alive. If Mastermind saves the Imp from execution, Scarlet Woman has no reason to activate — demon is still alive. The two backup demon-survival mechanics don't stack; Mastermind makes Scarlet Woman redundant in that moment.

title: Courtier can drunk the Vortox — stopping global corruption for 3 nights
description: Vortox makes all Townsfolk ability info wrong. If Courtier targets Vortox directly, Vortox is drunk for 3 nights — meaning their global false-info effect stops, and Townsfolk receive correct information again. One of the most powerful Courtier uses on a S&V script.

title: Philosopher as Savant: second round of two-fact questions
description: Philosopher copies the Savant. Each night, a new Savant-style pair of statements (one true, one false) arrives. If the real Savant is already sharing info, a Philosopher-Savant gives the town a second independent set of constrained facts to cross-reference.

title: Mathematician tracks which Savant statement was false
description: Savant gives one true and one false statement each night. Mathematician counts how many players received false ability info. When Mathematician's count rises by 1, it confirms the Savant was active that night — but also helps narrow which Savant statement was false.

title: Dreamer + Savant: two constrained information sources
description: Dreamer gives one player's character each night from two options. Savant gives one true and one false statement. Both provide information with built-in uncertainty. Cross-referencing Dreamer's character options against Savant's facts can resolve which option and which statement are correct.

title: No Dashii permanently poisons neighboring Savant
description: No Dashii's passive poison hits its Townsfolk neighbors. A Savant sitting next to No Dashii receives both statements as false — you choose which 'true' and which 'false' to show. The Savant has no trustworthy information, but doesn't know they're poisoned.

title: Pit-Hag can change the Mutant to a different character — ending Madness
description: Mutant's Outsider Madness requires them to claim to be an Outsider or risk dying. Pit-Hag can change the Mutant to a Townsfolk or different Outsider, removing their obligation. The Mutant suddenly no longer needs to maintain their cover story.

title: Barber swap can move the Mutant's character — ending Outsider Madness
description: Barber can swap two players' characters when it dies. If Mutant is swapped to a non-Outsider character, their Madness obligation ends — they no longer need to claim Outsider. The Mutant's strategic value as a Madness role is cancelled.

title: Devil's Advocate protects Klutz from execution death — Klutz still kills
description: Devil's Advocate lets one player survive execution. If Klutz is DA-protected and executed, Klutz doesn't die — but Klutz's ability still triggers: they must choose another player to die. Good loses a player from Klutz's trigger even though the Klutz survived. Double threat.

title: Poisoned Klutz executed: ability fails, no one chosen
description: Klutz's ability requires them to choose a player to die when executed. If Klutz is poisoned when executed, their ability is broken — no player is chosen to die. Poisoner targeting Klutz on the day they're executed completely neutralizes the Klutz's execution threat.

title: Gossip kills Klutz — does Klutz's ability trigger?
description: Klutz's ability triggers 'when you learn that you are dead.' If Gossip's true statement kills Klutz at night (not an execution), Klutz may still choose a player to die — triggering their ability even without being executed. Check your rulebook: night deaths may or may not trigger Klutz.

title: Flowergirl + Sage: track demon voting, then identify them
description: Flowergirl knows if the demon voted to execute each day. Sage, if killed by the demon, learns 2 players one of which is the demon. Cross-referencing: if Flowergirl confirms the demon voted on a specific day, Sage's two candidates narrow to whoever voted that day.

title: Flowergirl + Snake Charmer: narrow demon voters for the swap
description: Flowergirl knows if the demon voted today. Snake Charmer wants to target the demon. If Flowergirl confirms the demon voted, Snake Charmer can focus on players who voted — dramatically shrinking the pool for a snake-swap attempt.

title: Flowergirl + Oracle: demon behavior and dead evil tracking
description: Flowergirl tracks whether the demon voted (day behavior). Oracle counts dead evil players (night info). Together they profile the evil team over time: Oracle shows how many evil players have died, Flowergirl reveals when the demon is being bold or hiding.

title: Barber swap changes characters — Dreamer's accumulated info goes stale
description: Dreamer learns a player's character each night from two options. Barber can swap two players' characters when it dies. After a Barber death, the Dreamer's previous character info may now apply to the wrong player — careful tracking is essential.

title: Barber swap before execution: Undertaker sees the new character
description: Barber swaps two players' characters when it dies. If Barber dies just before an execution that same day, Undertaker sees the executed player's NEW character (after the swap), not their original. This can completely mislead the Undertaker's character tracking.

title: Barber moves the demon — Sage identifies the wrong player
description: Barber's death can swap the demon into a new body. Sage, if killed by the demon, learns 2 players including the current demon. After a Barber swap, previously gathered info about who the demon is may be wrong — the demon is now in a different player's seat.

title: Virgin chain-kills a Townsfolk — Professor can resurrect them
description: Virgin's nomination trigger can execute a good Townsfolk by accident. Professor can resurrect one dead Townsfolk. If Virgin causes a friendly-fire execution, Professor is the ideal recovery tool — bringing back the lost Townsfolk without spending a real execution.

title: Slayer misfires — Professor resurrects the victim
description: Slayer's one-shot kill might hit an innocent player. Professor can resurrect one dead Townsfolk. If Slayer kills the wrong person, Professor recovers the loss — giving the team resilience against one of the biggest risk moves good can take.
